3.happening: Creating an empty list for the spiral order.
3.why: Elements are collected as the cursor walks the grid.
3.invariant: result holds the elements in visit order.

4.why: The cursor needs the grid's height to detect walls.
4.invariant: m stays fixed for the whole walk.

5.why: The cursor needs the grid's width to detect walls.
5.invariant: n stays fixed for the whole walk.

6.happening: Preparing the 'seen' grid of visited flags.
6.why: Turning depends on knowing which cells were already visited.

7.happening: Building a flag row for matrix row {ri}.
7.why: Each matrix row needs its own row of flags.

8.happening: Starting the flag row as all not-visited.
8.why: No cell has been visited before the walk begins.

9.happening: Adding the flag row to the seen grid.
9.why: seen now covers one more matrix row.

10.happening: Listing the four clockwise directions: right, down, left, up.
10.why: Turning clockwise cycles through these in order.

11.happening: Placing the cursor on the top row.
11.why: The spiral starts at the top-left corner.
11.invariant: row is the cursor's current row.

12.happening: Placing the cursor on the left column.
12.why: The spiral starts at the top-left corner.
12.invariant: col is the cursor's current column.

13.why: The spiral first runs along the top row.

14.happening: Step {step} of the walk — one step per cell.
14.why: Exactly m*n steps fills the whole grid.
14.invariant: Every cell is visited exactly once.

15.happening: Visiting cell ({row}, {col}) = {matrix[row][col]} and adding it to the spiral.
15.why: The current cell's value joins the output in order.
15.invariant: result grows by one each step.

16.why: A visited cell must never be entered again.

17.happening: Looking ahead: the next row would be {row} + {directions[d][0]}.
17.why: We probe the next cell before committing to move.
17.invariant: next_row is only a candidate here.

18.happening: Looking ahead: the next column would be {col} + {directions[d][1]}.
18.why: We probe the next cell before committing to move.
18.invariant: next_col is only a candidate here.

19.happening: Testing whether ({next_row}, {next_col}) is outside the grid.
19.why: A cell off the grid means the cursor hit a wall.
19.invariant: out_of_bounds is true only past an edge.

20.happening: The next cell is off the grid or already visited — turn clockwise.
20.why: The spiral turns exactly when it cannot continue straight.
20.invariant: Turning keeps the path on unvisited cells.

20.happening: The next cell is open — keep moving straight.
20.why: No wall ahead, so the direction is unchanged.
20.invariant: The cursor continues along the current edge.

21.why: Right becomes down, down becomes left, and so on, wrapping around.
21.invariant: d advances by one, modulo four.

22.happening: Recomputing the next row after the turn.
22.why: The new direction points at a different candidate cell.
22.invariant: next_row now follows the turned direction.

23.happening: Recomputing the next column after the turn.
23.why: The new direction points at a different candidate cell.
23.invariant: next_col now follows the turned direction.

24.why: The chosen next cell becomes the current one.

25.why: The chosen next cell becomes the current one.

26.happening: Returning the {len(result)} elements in spiral order.
26.why: Every cell has been visited exactly once.

init.happening: Setting up the output, the visited grid, and the cursor.
init.why: The walk needs bounds, visited-tracking, and a starting cell.
init.invariant: seen mirrors the matrix; the cursor starts top-left.

loop.happening: Walking the cursor cell by cell.
loop.why: One step per cell traces the whole spiral.
loop.invariant: Each cell is visited exactly once.

update.happening: Recording a cell, marking it, and probing ahead.
update.why: Every step emits a value and looks for a wall.

check.happening: Deciding whether to turn or go straight.
check.why: A wall or a visited cell forces a clockwise turn.
check.invariant: The path stays on unvisited cells.

move.happening: Advancing the cursor to the next cell.
move.why: The cursor follows the current direction.
move.invariant: row and col move by one cell.

return.why: The whole grid has been visited.
